methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting pollen samples from small and shallow lakes on the Tibetan Plateau and processing them for analysis. The aim is to ensure the samples are representative and minimally influenced by external factors.

steps.description: Travel along hardened and dirt roads to collect samples from alpine meadow lakes on the Tibetan Plateau.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen samples from lakes on the Tibetan Plateau

steps.description: Select small and shallow lakes with less than 100 m radius and without long inflow rivers to collect pollen samples.
steps.searchable_summary: select appropriate lakes for pollen sample collection

steps.description: Collect lake surface sediment samples from the central part of each lake, with the top 2 cm of lake sediment forming the sample.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples from lake centers

steps.description: Subsample approximately 10 g of wet untreated sediment per sample for pollen extraction.
Process pollen samples using standard acid-alkali-acid procedures and 7 µm mesh sieving.
steps.searchable_summary: process pollen samples with acid-alkali-acid procedures

steps.description: Add a tablet with Lycopodium spores to each sample prior to pollen extraction as tracers.
steps.searchable_summary: add Lycopodium spores as tracers to samples

steps.description: Identify pollen grains with the aid of modern pollen reference slides and published atlases.
steps.searchable_summary: identify pollen grains using reference slides and atlases

steps.description: Count more than 500 terrestrial pollen grains and more than 200 Lycopodium spores for each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: count terrestrial pollen grains and Lycopodium spores

methods.description: This method involves obtaining climatic data for the sampled lakes, analyzing pollen data, and performing statistical analyses to understand the relationship between pollen assemblages and climatic variables.

steps.description: Obtain modern climatic data for the sampled lakes using the Chinese Meteorological Forcing Dataset (CMFD).
steps.searchable_summary: fetch climatic data from CMFD for sampled lakes

steps.description: Calculate geographical distances of each sampled lake to each pixel in the CMFD using the rdist.earth function.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate distances from lakes to CMFD pixels

steps.description: Assign meteorological data of the nearest pixel to a sampled lake to represent the climatic conditions of that lake.
steps.searchable_summary: assign nearest pixel meteorological data to lakes

steps.description: Calculate mean annual precipitation, mean annual temperature, and mean temperatures of the coldest and warmest months for each sampled lake.
Use ordination techniques to visualize relationships between modern pollen assemblages and climatic variables.
Perform redundancy analysis (RDA) to visualize the distribution of pollen species and sampling sites along climatic gradients.
steps.searchable_summary: perform RDA on pollen and climatic data

steps.description: Apply boosted regression tree (BRT) analysis to determine the influence of climatic variables on pollen taxa distribution.
steps.searchable_summary: apply BRT to analyze pollen-climate influence

steps.description: Run weighted-averaging partial least squares (WA-PLS) and random forest (RF) algorithms for past climate reconstruction.
steps.searchable_summary: perform WA-PLS and RF for climate reconstruction

steps.description: Perform statistical significance tests for WA-PLS and RF reconstructions.
steps.searchable_summary: validate WA-PLS and RF models with significance tests
